title: Level 14.1
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 14.1: "Child's Play"}} {{Rewrite|date=18/06/2026|Boring concept and doesn't have the best writing}} {{Class 1}} {{Background|file=lvl113h3.jpg}}{{VoidedTheme|mono}} '''Level 14.1''' is a sublevel of [[Level 14]]. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=lvl113h3.jpg|caption=A standard hallway in '''Level 14.1'''.|align=right}} '''Level 14.1''', also known as '''Child's Play''', appears to be a hospital, most likely made for children, that stretches out over a seemingly infinite distance. The hallways themselves consist of concrete walls painted in a light shade of gray with stripes stretching out at the bottom, being either green or purple. The floor is most likely made of concrete too, being in a stormy gray. The ceiling is similarly built to [[Level 0]], having fluorescent lights in inconsistent patterns, however, in this level they don't emit any sound, and there is a slim chance one light can be off. Chairs and tables are seldom in hallways, being set out in random layouts. Windows can also appear at the end of a hallway, however, they aren't harmful and don't show anything except a blinding white color. '''Level 14.1''' possesses a lot of closed and locked doors, sometimes being blocked by a chair or a table, with no way to enter them on the sides of its hallways. Travelers have reported trying to break in with a lockpick but to no avail. In a rare case, they can be entered into a standard hospital room. The rooms have the same walls and floor as in the corridors. The ceiling's look is also identical, however, none of the lights are on. The room commonly consists of three medium-sized hospital beds with metal handles on the sides, a modern-styled green armchair, and an unsystematic amount of chairs and tables. These things are set in a neat layout where none make contact with each other. The main light source of this area is the [[Entity 2|window]] set at the end of the room. It's strongly advised to stay away from it. '''Level 14.1's''' structure is puzzling, being incredibly unpredictable on every front. The level doesn't follow any layout, behaving like a labyrinth. The hallways can be in any arrangement that one can think of, a lot of them end in a dead-end with no way to progress further and stretch out for an unlimited variety of distances. A corridor can also be set from any angle, even if it looks like it shouldn't belong in its place. The level is devoid of any linearity, drawing more similarities from [[Level 0]]. However, unlike [[Level 0]], after a traveler walks through a corridor, it forever stays the same for that specific traveler. Navigating the level is effortless for an experienced explorer, as it has bright lightning everywhere. {{Blurimage|file=lvl113h2.jpg|caption='''A photo of a playroom'''.|align=right|width=275px|bg-align=right|font-size=15px}} Wanderers can rarely find playrooms when exploring the level. It's a spacious room with an overbearing amount of toys and games. The floor is made of a carpet-like material, being in various colors. The walls look identical to one in a hallway, same as the ceiling. When one enters this area, they may feel a sense of nostalgia. They might start reminiscing about their childhood. After a long period of staying in this area of the level, they will eventually fall in a state of insanity. A common thing one does at this stage is pretend as if they're a child. At this point of the effect, it's practically impossible to bring any person back to their usual self. '''Level 14.1''' can be potentially used as a habitable level, however it's advised against it. This fact mainly comes from various helpful objects in many areas of the level. The trash cans can hold a lot of items inside of them. The most common thing to find is food junk, like an empty milk carton or a banana peel, however, there is a chance of there being a normal banana and on very rare occasions, [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. There have also been reports of first aid kits and hand sanitizer bottles. Nonetheless, there have been cases of the objects being harmful or poisonous to travelers. == Entities == The only entity that can be found in '''Level 14.1''' are [[Entity 2|Windows]] which reside in most rooms. == Colonies And Outposts == The Nothing Colony * Population was last reported to be at 41 people. * Friendly and open to trade. * Looking for new members. [[M.E.G.]] Base Golfers * Consists of roughly 60 members. * Open to trade. * Won't accept anyone in. The Box Cult * Has a mere 20-30 members. * The group performs various rituals on its members, sometimes resulting in their death. * Extremely hostile; will attack a traveler if they spot one. * Very rarely accept anyone to join the group. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * Finding a teddy bear in [[Level 14]] will teleport you here. === Exits === * Finding a hallway with a pipe attached to the ceiling and following the pipe will eventually lead one into [[Level 15]]. * Going through a door labeled "Office" can sometimes teleport one to [[Level 4]]. * Finding a door with a kanji symbol and going through it leads one to [[Level 51]]. * If the level starts leaking water from the ceiling, start going in the direction of the water. After following it for long enough, all of the doors will turn blue and lead into [[Level 102]]. * Finding anesthesia and taking it will transport one into [[Level 16]].
